concerns.text: Task T017 specifies 'Euclidean distance in stoichiometry space' but omits the critical 'normalized element counts' constraint mandated by FR-003. Without explicit normalization, the distance metric will be dominated by stoichiometric magnitude rather than composition similarity, violating the FR-003 requirement for valid KNN imputation.

Tasks T028a and T028c implement a normality check on 'paired differences' and 'absolute errors' separately. FR-005 explicitly requires the Shapiro-Wilk test on the 'distribution of absolute errors' to determine the test type (t-test vs Wilcoxon). The task logic splits this into two separate checks and applies the decision to 'paired differences' (T028b), which contradicts the spec's decision logic for the statistical test selection.

Task T036 states 'Perform independent hyperparameter tuning' but relies on the dependency 'Depends on T026' (which saves the best model from the full grid search). This creates a semantic ambiguity where the reduced model might inherit the full model's hyperparameters rather than running a *new* grid search. SC-003 requires the reduced model to be 'trained independently' to measure feature redundancy; the task description fails to explicitly mandate a separate grid search execution, risking a violation of the success criterion.

Task T012 logs the exclusion of Materials Project and 2025 study data as a 'Scope Adjustment'. While the plan justifies this, the task does not mandate the creation of a formal 'Justification Document' or 'Change Request Record' artifact in `data/` or `outputs/`. This violates Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) and Principle IV (Single Source of Truth) regarding scope changes, as the override is only logged to stdout rather than recorded as a verifiable artifact.
